evals.prompt: I have a standalone SAPUI5 app running on our internal server and I need to apply our custom theme acme_dark to it. The UI Theme Designer runs at https://theming.acme.corp. How do I configure my app to use this theme?
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step instructions covering the theme origin allowlist meta tag, at least one of the three integration methods (URL params, bootstrap attrs, global config) with the correct theme root, the /UI5 suffix requirement, and versionedLibCss=true.

assertions.check: Response mentions sap-allowed-theme-origins as a required meta tag for the theme origin allowlist

assertions.description: Includes correct integration method with code
assertions.check: Response shows at least one correct method (URL params with sap-theme=, bootstrap data-sap-ui-theme, or globalThis sap-ui-config) with code example
Response explains that /UI5 must be appended to the theme root when using bootstrap attributes or global configuration
Response includes versionedLibCss=true as a required configuration
Response uses https://theming.acme.corp (from the prompt) in its examples, not a generic placeholder

evals.prompt: We've defined palette parameters in our custom theme for our brand colors (acmeBlue, acmeGreen). Now we want to use them in the CSS of our own custom SAPUI5 control com.acme.StatusBadge. The control has its own style.css bundled with the control library. How can we reference the theme colors in that CSS without hardcoding hex values?
evals.expected_output: Explains the CSS custom property bridge pattern: add custom CSS in the theme editor that exposes palette parameters as CSS custom properties via :root { --acmeBlue: @acmeBlue; }, then use var(--acmeBlue, fallback) in the control's style.css.

assertions.description: Shows :root CSS custom property bridge pattern
assertions.check: Response includes a code example with :root { --acmeBlue: @acmeBlue; } or similar pattern exposing palette params as CSS custom properties
Response mentions that the custom CSS editor uses LESS (specifically LESS 1.6.3 or just LESS), which is why @variable syntax works

assertions.description: Shows var() usage in control CSS
assertions.check: Response shows using var(--acmeBlue) or var(--acmeBlue, fallback) in the control's style.css
Response mentions that custom CSS must be added for the SAPUI5 UI technology specifically, or at least mentions selecting the right UI technology

evals.prompt: Our custom theme is based on sap_belize_hcb and we just upgraded our app to SAPUI5 1.136. The app now shows a completely different theme than what we designed. What's happening and how do we fix it?
evals.expected_output: Explains that Belize themes were removed in recent SAPUI5 versions, describes the theme fallback mechanism (the 5 phases), explains that the theme likely fell back to sap_horizon_hcb via the flavor fallback chain, and recommends migrating the custom theme to be based on a Horizon theme.

assertions.check: Response explains that Belize themes (sap_belize_hcb) were removed or are no longer supported in recent SAPUI5 versions like 1.136
Response describes the theme fallback mechanism — the multi-phase process the theming service uses when a theme is unavailable
Response identifies that the theme likely fell back to a Horizon variant (sap_horizon_hcb) or another HCB theme via the flavor fallback
Response recommends migrating the custom theme to be based on a Horizon or Quartz theme as the fix

evals.prompt: I'm building a custom tile component for our SAP Fiori app. Generate the CSS classes for the tile using SAP theming parameters so it looks consistent with the standard SAP Fiori tiles. The tile has a default state, hover state, active state, a title, and a subtitle. It should also have a border and rounded corners.
evals.expected_output: Generates CSS using var(--sapTile_*) CSS custom properties for background, border, border-radius, text colors, hover, and active states. Uses real SAP parameter names (not invented ones). Does NOT use hardcoded fallback values in var() — uses bare var(--sapXxx) instead. Uses rem for dimensions. Mentions the Parameter Explorer or parameter dependencies doc as a way to look up additional parameters. Mentions that the developer must ensure SAP theme CSS custom properties are available at runtime.

assertions.check: Response uses var(--sapTile_Background) for the tile background color
Response uses var(--sapTile_BorderColor) for the tile border color

assertions.description: Covers hover and active states with correct parameters
assertions.check: Response includes hover state using --sapTile_Hover_Background and/or active state using --sapTile_Active_Background

assertions.description: Does NOT use hardcoded fallback values in var()
assertions.check: Response uses bare var(--sapXxx) without hardcoded fallback values (NOT var(--sapXxx, #fff) or similar). It may mention that the developer must ensure the theme CSS is loaded instead.
Response uses rem units (not px) for dimension values like border-radius, padding, font-size, or spacing

assertions.description: References Parameter Explorer or parameter dependencies doc
assertions.check: Response mentions the Parameter Explorer (sap.github.io/theming-base-content) or the parameter dependencies documentation as a resource for looking up additional parameters

evals.prompt: I have a button component CSS file that uses hardcoded colors and our old design system's custom properties. Please migrate it to use SAP's design system CSS custom properties so it works with SAP theming (dark mode, high contrast, corporate branding). The file is at evals/button_premigration.css.
evals.expected_output: Migrated CSS replacing hardcoded values and legacy custom properties with var(--sapButton_*) and generic SAP parameters. Maps .btn-success to sapButton_Success_* (not Accept). Uses sapContent_DisabledOpacity for disabled opacity. Uses sapContent_FocusColor/FocusWidth/FocusStyle for focus outlines. Removes or annotates the :root block. Lists unmatched and unused properties.

assertions.check: Response maps .btn-success to --sapButton_Success_Background (or other sapButton_Success_* parameters), NOT to sapButton_Accept_* parameters

assertions.description: Uses sapContent_DisabledOpacity for disabled button opacity
assertions.check: Response uses var(--sapContent_DisabledOpacity) for the disabled state opacity instead of the hardcoded 0.5
Response uses var(--sapContent_FocusColor) and/or var(--sapContent_FocusWidth) for the focus/focus-visible outline instead of the hardcoded 2px solid #1A00EB
Response maps .btn-danger to --sapButton_Negative_Background (or other sapButton_Negative_* parameters), NOT to sapButton_Reject_* parameters

assertions.description: Removes or annotates the legacy :root block
assertions.check: Response removes the :root block with legacy custom properties or adds a comment explaining that SAP theming infrastructure provides these values

assertions.description: Uses bare var() without hardcoded fallback values
assertions.check: Response uses bare var(--sapXxx) without hardcoded fallback values (NOT var(--sapXxx, #fff) or similar)

For emphasized/success/negative/lite button variants, hover and active states use the variant-specific parameters (e.g. sapButton_Emphasized_Hover_Background, sapButton_Lite_Active_Background) rather than the base sapButton_Hover_* or sapButton_Active_*

assertions.description: Does not preserve hardcoded transparent for .btn-secondary background
assertions.check: For .btn-secondary, response uses var(--sapButton_Background) or another SAP parameter for background-color, NOT the hardcoded value 'transparent' from the original CSS

evals.prompt: Migrate this button CSS to SAP design system parameters: evals/button_premigration.css. After the migration, list any legacy custom properties that couldn't be mapped to an SAP equivalent, and list SAP button parameters that are relevant but not used in the migrated CSS.
evals.expected_output: Migrated CSS plus two lists: (1) unmatched legacy properties including --button-border-style which has no SAP equivalent, and (2) unused SAP button parameters that could be added for more comprehensive theming.

assertions.check: Response identifies --button-border-style as a legacy custom property that has no SAP equivalent
Response lists at least 2 SAP button parameters that are relevant to buttons but not used in the migrated CSS
Response maps .button-primary to --sapButton_Emphasized_Background or other sapButton_Emphasized_* parameters

assertions.description: Uses complete parameter sets per variant (component-first)
assertions.check: For at least one variant (e.g. emphasized), the migration includes parameters beyond what existed in the original CSS, such as font-family, text-shadow, or font-weight specific to that variant — showing a component-first approach rather than 1:1 property replacement

assertions.description: Summarizes unused parameters by family, not individual params
assertions.check: The unused SAP parameters section groups or summarizes parameters by family/namespace (e.g. 'sapButton_Accept_*') rather than listing every individual parameter one by one

evals.prompt: I need to migrate my button CSS to SAP theming parameters. My CSS has these button variants: .btn (default), .button-primary (main CTA), .btn-success (positive feedback state), .btn-danger (error/destructive), .btn-warning (caution), .btn-link (transparent/text-only). Which SAP button parameter families should each variant map to? Explain the reasoning, especially for any non-obvious mappings.
evals.expected_output: Mapping table with correct semantic assignments: default -> sapButton_, primary -> sapButton_Emphasized_, success -> sapButton_Success_ (NOT Accept), danger -> sapButton_Negative_, warning -> sapButton_Critical_, link -> sapButton_Lite_. Explains the Accept vs Success distinction.

assertions.check: Response maps .button-primary (main CTA) to sapButton_Emphasized_* parameters

assertions.description: Maps success to Success, not Accept
assertions.check: Response maps .btn-success (positive feedback state) to sapButton_Success_* parameters and does NOT map it to sapButton_Accept_*

assertions.description: Maps danger to Negative, not Reject
assertions.check: Response maps .btn-danger (error/destructive) to sapButton_Negative_* parameters and does NOT map it to sapButton_Reject_*
Response maps .btn-warning (caution) to sapButton_Critical_* parameters
Response maps .btn-link (transparent/text-only) to sapButton_Lite_* parameters
Response explicitly explains the difference between Accept (action: confirming/accepting) and Success (state: positive feedback) in SAP button semantics

assertions.description: Explains Warning vs Critical follows the same pattern
assertions.check: Response explains that Warning (action) vs Critical (state) follows the same action/state distinction as Accept/Success and Reject/Negative

evals.prompt: Please migrate this card component CSS to SAP theming parameters so it works with dark mode and high contrast. The file is at evals/card_premigration.css.
evals.expected_output: Migrated CSS replacing hardcoded values with var(--sapTile_*) and generic SAP parameters. SAP uses sapTile_* for both tiles and cards (no sapCard_* family). Uses sapContent_Shadow* for box-shadow. Uses sapContent_DisabledOpacity for disabled. Removes or annotates the :root block. Lists unmatched and unused properties.

assertions.check: Response uses var(--sapTile_Background) for the card background color (SAP uses sapTile_* for both tiles and cards — there is no sapCard_* family)
Response uses var(--sapTile_BorderColor) for the card border color
Response uses var(--sapContent_Shadow0) or var(--sapContent_Shadow1) or another sapContent_Shadow* parameter for the card box-shadow instead of the hardcoded rgba shadow
Response uses var(--sapTile_SeparatorColor) for the internal header/footer border colors
Response uses var(--sapFontFamily) or var(--sapFontHeaderFamily) instead of the hardcoded 'Segoe UI' font stack

assertions.description: Removes or annotates the legacy :root block
assertions.check: Response removes the :root block with legacy custom properties or adds a comment explaining that SAP theming provides these values

assertions.description: Uses bare var() without hardcoded fallback values
assertions.check: Response uses bare var(--sapXxx) without hardcoded fallback values
Response uses var(--sapContent_DisabledOpacity) for the .card.disabled opacity instead of the hardcoded 0.5

evals.prompt: I have a form field CSS file with input styles, value states (error, warning, success), disabled and read-only states. Migrate it to use SAP theming parameters. The file is at evals/field_premigration.css.
evals.expected_output: Migrated CSS replacing hardcoded values with var(--sapField_*) and generic SAP parameters. Maps value states to sapField_Error_*, sapField_Warning_*, sapField_Success_*. Uses sapContent_DisabledOpacity and sapContent_FocusColor. Removes or annotates the :root block.

assertions.check: Response uses var(--sapField_Background) for the base input field background color
Response uses var(--sapField_BorderColor) for the base input field border color
Response maps .is-error to sapField error parameters like var(--sapField_InvalidColor) or var(--sapField_InvalidBackground) or similar sapField_* error parameters
Response maps .is-warning to sapField warning parameters like var(--sapField_WarningColor) or var(--sapField_WarningBackground) or similar sapField_* warning parameters
Response maps .is-success to sapField success parameters like var(--sapField_SuccessColor) or var(--sapField_SuccessBackground) or similar sapField_* success parameters
Response uses var(--sapContent_DisabledOpacity) for the disabled state opacity instead of the hardcoded 0.6
Response uses var(--sapFontFamily) instead of the hardcoded 'Arial' font

assertions.description: Removes or annotates the legacy :root block
assertions.check: Response removes the :root block with legacy custom properties or adds a comment explaining that SAP theming provides these values
Response uses var(--sapField_Hover_BorderColor) or similar hover parameter for the field hover state border color
